user_ask.pull_all_the_data: ALREADY DEFAULT-ON — the real failure was a COLD CACHE (M217's cache prime), not a missing default. Documented (S0).
user_ask.seed_the_3_story_orgs: ALREADY DEFAULT-ON — the preset ships 3 (Cervato / Solvantis / Northwind). The docs LIED ('2 orgs') at 11 sites, live 4 releases. Fixed + fenced (S0).
user_ask.remote_access_opt_out_for_demo: THE ONE GENUINE FLIP. Landed (S3): a bare up-injected.sh 1 auto-discovers the tailnet host via a 6-rung capability ladder and serves over a trusted cert. Opt out with --no-public-host. Proven live on billion (hero login -> /profile 200; manager -> /enterprise/workforce 200).

sections.S0: the two doc lies fixed + 3 doc-vs-code fences (org count 11 violations, exposure claim 3 false + 2 missing, defaults table)
sections.S1: safety.md Part 3 — the exposure axis + the explicit supersession of v2.2's D-DESIGN-1
sections.S2: the /demo-up defaults contract (26 knobs + 10 flags, derived from the parsers, 2 entry points)
sections.S3: remote reach DEFAULT-ON via the 6-rung ladder (opt-out) — proven live on billion, both vantages
sections.S4: the presenter cockpit fronted behind the trusted cert (was the one plain-HTTP unauthenticated surface)
sections.S5: the academy stops POISONING the demo session (Clerkenstein-wired) + studio-desk was never a session bug (D15)
sections.S6: zero egress on an authenticated load (7 third parties killed) + clerk-js served from disk (bounded, was unbounded CDN)
sections.S7: the dev-side OPT-IN --public-host (Fate 1, folds+discharges M216) + the dev family's 0.0.0.0 disclosure it never had

tests.python_delta_vs_m219: +312 COUNTED, but the honest number is smaller: M219 counted 903 across demo-stack/stack-injection/stack-verify/stack-core ONLY, because the dev-stack suite could not be run whole (rc=124). This close FIXED that (D31), so dev-stack (120) is countable for the first time and the other sections' M220 additions land on top. Like-for-like on the 4 sections M219 counted: 583+246+152+114 = 1095 vs 903 = +192; plus dev-stack 120 now visible.
tests.python_counting_method: JUnit XML (pytest --junit-xml), classname-grouped from the testsuite element. NEVER grepped from piped stdout.
tests.whole_python_suite_first_completion: This is the FIRST milestone at which `python3.12 -m pytest` over the whole rext tree completes at all — the dev-stack suite busy-failed (rc=124) for 5 milestones across 2 releases until D31.
tests.go_counting_method: grep -c '^func Test' across all 6 go.mod modules — the SAME method M217/M218/M219 recorded, so the +6 is like-for-like.

coverage_harden_pass.exposure_claim_guard.py: 57% -> 89% (the S7 DEV half + all of main() were uncovered; delete _DEV_DISCLOSURE_RE and the suite stayed green)
coverage_harden_pass.tailscale_autohost.py: 92% -> 99% (_default_run's never-raises arms + the new rung-3 hostname refusal)
coverage_harden_pass.gen_tailscale_serve.py: 81% -> 94% (reset_script was 100% uncovered while S4's :7700-in-reset claim was load-bearing)
coverage_harden_pass.dev_flag_guard.py: 71% -> 74% (incl. the anti-D17 'zero flags must FAIL' branch)

mutation_battery.artifact: stack-core/tests/test_m220_mutation_battery.py (COMMITTED — S3/S7 claimed 12+9 mutants but committed NEITHER battery; the result survived only as a sentence in a progress note, which is the exact D17 the release is named for)
mutation_battery.breakdown: 4 naive ladders (in-process) + 6 up-injected.sh + 7 dev-stack, driven through the REAL suites via two env seams
anti_theatre_assertions: the BASELINE must be GREEN before any RED is read (fired on its own first run — caught a 14-test staging bug that would have logged all 7 dev mutants as false REDs, S7's original bug reproduced live)
every mutant must ACTUALLY change its subject (a no-op mutant that 'goes RED' measures nothing)
the failure SIGNATURES must not all be identical (a constant is not a result — the tell that nearly let S7's broken battery through)

findings.H-1: THE HARD INVARIANT WAS UNFENCED — the fence RE-TYPED the SCHEME/BIND_HOST derivation inside the test, so mutating the shipped SCHEME to unconditional https left all 23 tests green. Fixed by naming the derivation (derive_public_host_vars) so the fence calls the shipped one. Mutants D5/D6 now RED.
findings.H-3: the || true in resolve_dev_public_host was load-bearing and untested (mutant V4 survived) — a broken tailscale can never exercise it (ladder is exit-0-always); only a broken ladder PROCESS can. New regression test breaks the process.

flake_gate: 5 sequential runs of the milestone-touched suites in random order — all identical, 0 flakes

demo_patches.added_this_milestone: next-web-no-thirdparty (S6/g — gates 4 hardcoded ad/analytics scripts = 7 third parties behind one build-time env var; behaviour-identical when unset)

deferrals.chronic_resolved: the dev-stack suite — 5 milestones / 2 releases as 'environmental' — LANDED Fate 1 (one env var, D31)
deferrals.m221_stale_route_corrected: FIX-M221-devstack-test-spin was DISCHARGED (its 'busy-spins forever' description was already known-wrong); M221 overview.md retraction written rather than a silent delete

notes: M220 delivered: the user's ask was three things, and TWO were already true — the docs lied. 'Pull all data' + 'seed 3 orgs' were both default-on; the '2 orgs' lie (11 sites, 4 releases) was why the user believed the ask was unmet. The one genuine flip (remote opt-out) landed and is live-proven. The invariant that mattered most and holds: a box with no Tailscale falls back to BYTE-IDENTICAL localhost behaviour (proven by genuinely removing tailscale, tripwire-fenced). D17 was the retro's spine (~20 hits M217->M220), several found by the tooling IN ITSELF. NOT the final milestone — M221 is last; no merge to main, no v2.3 tag.
